scores.rationale: It clearly prioritizes the business off-ramp journey while preserving a usable integrator path and request-access flow.
Home, developer overview, and request access share a coherent audience hierarchy, navigation path, and responsive strategy.
The candidate gives detailed per-screen states, mobile rules, field behavior, code containment, and recovery requirements.
It defines a distinct business-first information hierarchy and rejects unsupported or proprietary visual and product claims.
It covers keyboard paths, programmatic validation, focus movement, text fallbacks, draft preservation, and success/error announcements.
The brief-only boundary and unsupported-claims guardrail are explicit, though the source decisions do not cite corpus records.
